nature: Humanitix remittance of $1,150 — event-ticketing platform paying through ticket sales to ACT. This is event/ticket income (fee-for-service), not a grant.
your_comment: Humanitix $1,150 Interbank Credit = event-ticketing platform remitting ticket-sales income to ACT — this is event/ticket revenue (fee-for-service), NOT a grant. Code as Other Revenue (event income), project ACT-IN as the general bucket pending event confirmation. The Dext "Humanitix - 2025-11-05 - HPJCFBWSB5.pdf" receipt is a GENUINE match (ref code HPJCFBWSB5 ties to the line particulars), so receipt is supported — but it is a platform remittance statement, treat as nature-confirmation evidence, not a clean per-ticket tax invoice. TWO things to confirm before locking GST: (1) Net-vs-gross — Humanitix usually remits ticket sales NET of its booking/platform fee, so $1,150 is likely the net figure. Income GST should be 1/11th of the GROSS ticket sales, with the Humanitix fee booked separately (its own GST), NOT simply 1/11th of $1,150. Pull the Humanitix settlement statement to split gross sales vs fee before posting. (2) GST on Income only applies if the sole trader was GST-registered and the event was a taxable supply at the time — confirm registration status for the period. If both confirmed, GST on Income is correct; otherwise GST-free. Grant-vs-revenue call and GST direction (on income) are right; the open item is the gross/net treatment and the registration check.
verify_note: Refute result: revenue-vs-grant correct (revenue, not grant); account bucket OK (ACT-IN/Other Revenue, event TBC). Disagree on two points: receipt_status ON_FILE_CONFIRMED is too strong (it is a platform remittance statement, not a per-ticket tax invoice — genuine match though, ref HPJCFBWSB5 ties out, NOT a mismatch); and GST treatment ignores the net-vs-gross issue — Humanitix typically remits net of platform fee, so 1/11th of $1,150 likely understates gross income GST and omits the fee with its own GST. Also GST-on-income presumes sole-trader GST registration for the period (not in evidence). Not a grant error, not a P2P/personal error — a GST-mechanics gap, so agree=false pending the settlement statement split + registration check.
